summary: The SARS-related viruses—including both SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2—bind to the human ACE2 receptor through their spike proteins’ receptor-binding domains, facilitating cell entry. This mechanism has been validated experimentally and clinically, forming the basis for antiviral therapies aimed at blocking such interactions.
